# Kevin Lira

> Computer engineer and Ph.D. student in Computer Science at North Carolina State University (USA), researching large language models (LLMs) for automated detection and repair of software vulnerabilities.

Kevin Lira leads initiatives on performance, usability, and secure development workflows, and studies practical, scalable approaches to applying LLMs to real-world codebases for vulnerability detection and repair.

## Pages

- [Homepage](https://kevinws.com/): Bio, education, research focus, and contact information.
- [Publications](https://kevinws.com/publications.html): Peer-reviewed papers, education history, and patents.

## Research

- Vulnerability Detection with Interprocedural Context in Multiple Languages: Assessing Effectiveness and Cost of Modern LLMs — 30th International Conference on Evaluation and Assessment in Software Engineering (EASE), AI Models/Data track, 2026.
- Beyond Code Explanations: A Ray of Hope for Cross-Language Vulnerability Repair — 2nd ACM International Conference on AI-powered Software (AIware), 2025.
- Turning Manual Tasks into Actions: Assessing the Effectiveness of Gemini-generated Selenium Tests — 2nd ACM International Conference on AI-powered Software (AIware), 2025.
